expected.notes: seed 003-janrosenow: heat pumps outsold gas boilers GB

postText:
For the first time ever, heat pumps outsold gas boilers in Germany in 2025.

48% of all new heating systems installed last year were heat pumps. Gas fell to just 39%. A decade ago, heat pumps had a 7% market share. The transformation has been remarkable. https://t.co/wWX35d5yqE
bundle.chartTitle: Heat pumps overtake gas in Germany's heating market
keyFindings: Heat pumps are projected to reach a 48% share of new heating installations in Germany by 2025, overtaking gas boilers for the first time.
The market share of gas boilers remained stable above 65% from 2010 to 2021 but is expected to decline sharply to roughly 39% by 2025.
Heat pump adoption saw a major inflection point in 2022, jumping from 17% in 2021 to 24% and continuing its rapid growth thereafter.
The combined share of oil boilers and biomass/other heating systems has steadily contracted as low-carbon electric alternatives gained dominance.

sourceLines.sourceText: Jan Rosenow | Source: BDH (Federal Association of the German Heating Industry); BWP; TGA-Praxis

publisherHints.label: BDH (Federal Association of the German Heating Industry)
